$schema_description: NARA CUI Registry categories relevant to Mycosoft defense contracting, for the compliance web app's L3 pre-readiness and CUI-handling surfaces. Category markings and banner text are exact per the NARA CUI Registry per task DISCIPLINE rule.

categories.relevance_to_mycosoft: Navy TAC-O statement-of-work-derived technical materials and DARPA program data are Mycosoft's primary anticipated CUI category.

Undersea sensor technology and autonomous-platform work may qualify as export-controlled technical data under ITAR or EAR, creating a live overlap risk with Mycosoft's Psathyrella buoy and related sensor programs.
Contract cost data, pricing information, and proprietary technical/business information shared with or generated for the government under Mycosoft's Navy/DARPA/SBIR contracts.

authorities.authority: 44 U.S.C. 3554 (see OMB M-24-04 for implementing guidance)

categories.category_description: Related to information that if not protected, could result in adverse effects to information systems. Information system means a discrete set of information resources organized for the collection, processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination, or disposition of information.
categories.relevance_to_mycosoft: Applies to vulnerability assessment findings, penetration test results (required under CMMC L3 control CA.L3-3.12.1e), and system security documentation Mycosoft generates internally or receives from assessors.

categories.category_description: Applies where a law, regulation, or government-wide policy requires or permits safeguarding of the information but does not prescribe specific controls, and no more specific NARA Registry category/subcategory applies. Governed by the general CUI Basic baseline established under 32 CFR Part 2002 rather than a category-specific 'SP-' authority.
categories.relevance_to_mycosoft: Baseline handling requirement for any defense-related information Mycosoft receives or generates that does not fall into CTI, EXPT, PROPIN, or ISVI specifically, per Mycosoft's own CMMC scope documentation.

basic_vs_specified_definitions.cui_basic: Applies where the law, regulation, or government-wide policy requires or permits safeguarding but does not prescribe specific handling controls — the CUI Program's uniform baseline set of controls (32 CFR Part 2002) governs.
basic_vs_specified_definitions.cui_specified: Applies where the underlying authority itself prescribes specific handling controls that impose more stringent, different, or additional controls than the CUI Basic baseline (e.g., ITAR/EAR authorities for EXPT, DFARS 252.204-7012 for CTI, FAR 52.203-13 for PROPIN).
basic_vs_specified_definitions.citation: 32 CFR 2002.4(h) (definitional basis, as cross-referenced in the CMMC Assessment Guide)

marking_rules.banner_marking: Placed at the top (and generally bottom) of a document to indicate overall CUI status of the document, e.g., CUI, CUI//SP-CTI, CUI//SP-EXPT.
marking_rules.portion_marking: Individual sections, paragraphs, or fields within a document may carry their own category marking so only the CUI-bearing portions are flagged, distinct from the banner marking of the whole document.
marking_rules.decontrolling: Governed by 32 CFR 2002.18. Agencies should decontrol CUI as soon as practicable once safeguarding is no longer required, either automatically (e.g., on a pre-determined event or date per 32 CFR 2002.20(g)) or by affirmative agency decision. Decontrol relieves CUI Program handling obligations but does not itself authorize public release (2002.18(e)); authorized holders must clearly indicate formerly-CUI information is no longer controlled when reusing/republishing it (2002.18(f)); unauthorized disclosure does not constitute decontrol (2002.18(j)); agencies may not decontrol information to conceal or evade accountability for an unauthorized disclosure (2002.18(k)).

governing_framework.executive_order: EO 13556, Controlled Unclassified Information (November 2010) — establishes the CUI Program and designates NARA as Executive Agent.
governing_framework.implementing_regulation: 32 CFR Part 2002, Controlled Unclassified Information — establishes CUI categories, marking, safeguarding, dissemination, and decontrol rules government-wide.
